summary: Postinstall drops and executes remote Windows binary from unrelated personal GitHub repo
details: Package advertises itself as a text-utility library but ships a postinstall hook (`node install.js`) that fires on `npm install`. On Windows hosts, install.js base64-decodes a hardcoded URL pointing at an unrelated personal GitHub user's repo (raw.githubusercontent.com/cphc811-ui/d3d/main/npm-sc-legit.exe) on a mutable `main` branch, writes a PowerShell script to %TEMP%, and invokes `powershell.exe -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Exec Bypass -File <ps1>` via execFileSync to download and execute the.exe hidden, with no hash or signature verification. The script early-exits when `process.platform!== 'win32'` or `NODE_ENV === 'development'`, hiding execution from non-Windows CI and developer workstations. The fetched binary's purpose is unrelated to the advertised package function.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
